name: probetheus

metadata.name: probetheus
metadata.summary: A Python package to design probes against overrepresented sequences in a fastq file.

documentation:
# PROBETHEUS

A Python package to detect overrepresented sequences in a fastq file and design probes against them. Designed for single read sequencing from immunoprecipitation experiments, riboSeq, and other single read sequencing experiments.

## Installation

```bash
pip install probetheus
```

## Features

- Process single-end FASTQ files to find top represented sequences
- Generate probes from top sequences with customizable lengths
- Cluster sequences based on edit distance
- Detect probe binding sites against reference sequences
- Generate cumulative percentage plots with elbow point detection
- Reanalyze results with custom elbow points
- Subsample input files for faster analysis or testing

## Usage

### Processing FASTQ Files and Generating Probes

```bash
# Basic usage
probetheus process --input input.fastq.gz --output results.tsv

# With core sequence analysis
probetheus process --input input.fastq.gz --output results.tsv --find_core --core_length 25

# Process without length filtering
probetheus process --input input.fastq.gz --output results.tsv --skip_length_filter

# Check probe binding against reference
probetheus process --input input.fastq.gz --output results.tsv --reference ref.fasta --max_binding_dist 2

# Process with subsampling (e.g., use 10% of reads)
probetheus process --input input.fastq.gz --output results.tsv --subsample 10
```

### Reanalyzing Results

After initial processing, you can reanalyze the results with a different elbow point:

```bash
# Reanalyze with a new elbow point
probetheus reanalyze --input results.tsv --elbow 5 --output-prefix new_results
```

This will create:
- `new_results_reanalyzed.tsv`: New results file with selected sequences
- `new_results_reanalyzed_cumulative.png`: Updated cumulative plot

## Arguments

### Process Command
- `--input`, `-i`: Input FASTQ files (can be multiple)
- `--output`, `-o`: Output table file
- `--min-length`: Minimum sequence length (default: 20)
- `--max-length`: Maximum sequence length (default: 50)
- `--top-n`: Number of top sequences to use for probe generation (default: 20)
- `--probe-length`: Length of generated probes (default: 25)
- `--min-probe-length`: Minimum acceptable probe length (default: 20)
- `--edit-distance`: Maximum edit distance for clustering (default: 1)
- `--find-core`: Find core sequences
- `--core-length`: Length for core sequence analysis (default: 25)
- `--min-core-occurrence`: Minimum fraction of sequences a core must appear in (default: 0.1)
- `--reference`, `-r`: Reference FASTA file to check probe binding
- `--max-binding-dist`: Maximum edit distance allowed for probe binding (default: 2)
- `--subsample`: Subsample percentage (1-100) of reads from each file

### Reanalyze Command
- `--input`, `-i`: Input results.tsv file from previous analysis
- `--elbow`, `-e`: New elbow point (number of sequences to keep)
- `--output-prefix`, `-o`: Prefix for output files (optional)

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

modules.name: probetheus
documentation: PROBETHEUS: A tool for processing and analyzing sequence data with probe binding site detection.

modules.name: probetheus.cli
documentation: Command-line interface for PROBETHEUS.

functions.name: probetheus.cli.cli
documentation: PROBETHEUS: Process and analyze sequence data with probe binding site detection.

functions.name: probetheus.cli.process
documentation: Process FASTQ files to find and analyze sequences.

functions.name: probetheus.cli.reanalyze
documentation: Reanalyze results with a new elbow point.

functions.name: probetheus.cli.main
documentation: Main entry point for the command-line interface.

modules.name: probetheus.core
documentation: Core functionality for sequence processing and analysis.

functions.name: probetheus.core.chunk_list
documentation: Split a list into n chunks of approximately equal size.

functions.name: probetheus.core.align_sequence_chunk
documentation: Process a chunk of sequences for clustering.

functions.name: probetheus.core.cluster_sequences
documentation: Cluster sequences based on k-mer similarity.

functions.name: probetheus.core.get_kmers
documentation: Get k-mers from a sequence.

functions.name: probetheus.core.check_probe_binding_chunk
documentation: Process a chunk of sequences for probe binding.

functions.name: probetheus.core.write_binding_results
documentation: Write probe binding results to a file using parallel processing.

functions.name: probetheus.core.read_fastq_files
documentation: Read and process FASTQ files on-the-fly.

functions.name: probetheus.core.filter_by_length
documentation: Filter sequences by length.

functions.name: probetheus.core.calculate_percentages
documentation: Calculate percentages for each cluster.

functions.name: probetheus.core.find_elbow_point
documentation: Find the elbow point in the curve using the kneedle algorithm.

functions.name: probetheus.core.plot_cumulative_percentages
documentation: Create a cumulative percentage plot with elbow point.

functions.name: probetheus.core.find_core_sequences
documentation: Find core sequences through multiple sequence alignment.

functions.name: probetheus.core.process_alignment
documentation: Process the multiple sequence alignment to find conserved regions.

functions.name: probetheus.core.read_reference_sequences
documentation: Read reference sequences from a FASTA file.

functions.name: probetheus.core.check_probe_binding
documentation: Check if a probe sequence binds to a reference sequence.

functions.name: probetheus.core.reanalyze_results
documentation: Reanalyze results with a new elbow point.

functions.name: probetheus.core.generate_probes
documentation:
Generate probes of specified length from a sequence.

Args:
    sequence: Input DNA sequence
    probe_length: Desired length of probes (default: 25)
    min_probe_length: Minimum acceptable probe length (default: 20)

Returns:
    List of probe sequences

functions.name: probetheus.core.write_probe_sequences
documentation: Generate and write probe sequences from top sequences.

variables.name: probetheus.core.CPUS
documentation: Leave one CPU free for system
